Title: The soybean Rhg1 amino acid transporter gene alters glutamate homeostasis and jasmonic acid-induced resistance to soybean cyst nematode

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glutamate acts in nitrogen transport/homeostasis and signalling; jasmonates function in defence and contribute to resistance to soybean cyst nematode.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots (glutamate, JA, JA-Ile); leaf phloem exudates for transported glutamate
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Amino acid transporter family protein with amino acid transporter domain (aa 22–426); homologous to Arabidopsis AVT6C/AVT6D
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitous; highest in roots, stems, and cotyledons; promoter active mainly in vasculature, strong in root stele/pericycle
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous; highest in roots, stems, and cotyledons; promoter active in vasculature
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes shoot-to-root glutamate transport, elevates root glutamate, activates jasmonic acid pathway, and contributes to JA-associated SCN resistance context, but overexpression alone does not confer SCN resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis and soybean; near-isogenic line comparison; promoter-GUS analysis; GFP subcellular localization; amino acid toxicity assays with glutamate and MSX; HPLC-MS/MS amino acid profiling of phloem/xylem/roots/leaves; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; targeted JA/JA-Ile quantification; SCN infection assay with JA biosynthesis inhibitor nPG
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression/localization; native Rhg1-GmAAT promoter for GUS
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (roots of WT vs Rhg1-GmAAT-OX; accession NA); metabolite profiling/targeted HPLC-MS/MS for amino acids and jasmonates (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous glutamate; methionine sulfoximine; JA biosynthesis inhibitor n-propyl gallate; SCN infection
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression alone did not provide SCN resistance despite jasmonate activation; broad metabolic shifts included suppression of many other metabolites/amino acids.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as one component of the Rhg1 resistance locus; should be combined with the other Rhg1 genes rather than used alone for engineering SCN resistance; may be leveraged to modulate root glutamate/JA homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct glutamate transport activity was not biochemically demonstrated, but multiple in planta assays strongly supported a role in glutamate transport/homeostasis. It is one of three Rhg1 genes required together for full SCN resistance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rhg1-GmAAT encodes a predicted amino acid transporter expressed mainly in vascular tissues and localized to the plasma membrane and nucleus. 2. Overexpression specifically increased tolerance to toxic glutamate/MSX and increased glutamate-associated sensor gene expression, supporting altered glutamate transport/homeostasis. 3. Rhg1-GmAAT overexpression enhanced shoot-to-root glutamate transport, increased root glutamate, and reduced leaf glutamate. 4. Rhg1-GmAAT overexpression activated JA biosynthesis/signalling genes and increased JA and JA-Ile levels in roots. 5. Inhibiting JA biosynthesis reduced SCN resistance in Rhg1-containing PI88788, linking the Rhg1-GmAAT-associated JA pathway to resistance context. 6. Rhg1-GmAAT alone was insufficient to confer SCN resistance, consistent with the need for multiple Rhg1 genes.
